banner.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1200 360" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Saturday Questions Basic SQL"><defs><linearGradient id="g" x1="0" y1="0" x2="1" y2="1"><stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ff9f1c"/><stop offset="100%" stop-color="#ff7b00"/></linearGradient></defs><rect width="1200" height="360" fill="url(#g)"/><circle cx="1040" cy="80" r="140" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.12)"/><circle cx="1120" cy="300" r="210" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.08)"/><text x="60" y="135" font-size="58" font-weight="900" fill="white">Saturday Questions ❓</text><text x="60" y="195" font-size="36" font-weight="800" fill="white">Basic SQL</text><text x="60" y="245" font-size="22" font-weight="600" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.92)">SELECT • WHERE • ORDER BY • DISTINCT</text><text x="60" y="295" font-size="20" font-weight="800" fill="white">Saturday, 02-05-2026</text><g font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="white"><rect x="770" y="100" width="360" height="170" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.18)"/><text x="810" y="145">SELECT name, age</text><text x="810" y="185">FROM students</text><text x="810" y="225">WHERE city='Lucknow';</text></g><text x="815" y="300" font-size="22" font-weight="800" fill="white">Query • Filter • Sort</text></svg>

questions.title: What is SQL?
questions.prompt:
What does SQL stand for?

Choose the correct answer:
A. Structured Question Language
B. Structured Query Language
C. Simple Query Logic
D. System Query Language
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1000 340" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="What is SQL"><rect width="1000" height="340" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.74)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="50" font-size="28" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">What is SQL?</text><text x="30" y="90" font-size="18" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">SQL is the language used to talk to databases.</text><rect x="70" y="130" width="360" height="120" rx="20" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.16)"/><text x="100" y="178" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">S</text><text x="140" y="178" font-size="20" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Structured</text><text x="100" y="218" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Q</text><text x="140" y="218" font-size="20" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Query</text><text x="100" y="258" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">L</text><text x="140" y="258" font-size="20" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Language</text><rect x="560" y="130" width="340" height="120" rx="20" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.12)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="590" y="180" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Example</text><text x="590" y="220" font-size="20" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">SELECT * FROM students;</text></svg>
hints: It is not Structured Question Language.
questions.starter_code: -- Write the full form of SQL
questions.solution_code: -- SQL stands for Structured Query Language

questions.title: Select All Records
questions.prompt: Write an SQL query to select all columns and all rows from a table named students.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1000 340" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Select all records"><rect width="1000" height="340" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.74)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="50" font-size="28" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">SELECT All Records</text><text x="30" y="90" font-size="18" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">The star symbol means all columns.</text><rect x="70" y="130" width="420" height="90" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.16)"/><text x="100" y="185" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">SELECT * FROM students;</text><rect x="580" y="120" width="300" height="150" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.9)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="610" y="160" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">students</text><text x="610" y="200" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">id | name | age | city</text><text x="610" y="235" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">1 | Asha | 18 | Lucknow</text></svg>
hints: Use FROM before the table name.

questions.title: Select Specific Columns
questions.prompt: Write an SQL query to select only name and age from the students table.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1000 340" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="Select specific columns"><rect width="1000" height="340" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.74)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="50" font-size="28" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Select Specific Columns</text><text x="30" y="90" font-size="18" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">Mention only the columns you need.</text><rect x="70" y="130" width="500" height="90" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.16)"/><text x="100" y="185" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">SELECT name, age FROM students;</text><rect x="650" y="120" width="250" height="150" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.12)"/><text x="690" y="170" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Output Columns</text><text x="700" y="215" font-size="18" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">name</text><text x="700" y="250" font-size="18" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">age</text></svg>
hints: Write column names separated by commas.

questions.title: Filter Records with WHERE
questions.prompt:
Write an SQL query to get all students whose city is Lucknow.

Table: students
Columns: id, name, age, city
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1000 340" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="WHERE filter"><rect width="1000" height="340" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.74)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="50" font-size="28" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">WHERE Filter</text><text x="30" y="90" font-size="18" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">WHERE returns only matching rows.</text><rect x="60" y="130" width="520" height="100" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.16)"/><text x="90" y="175" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">SELECT * FROM students</text><text x="90" y="210" font-size="22" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">WHERE city = 'Lucknow';</text><rect x="650" y="120" width="280" height="150" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.9)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="680" y="165" font-size="18" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Matching rows</text><text x="680" y="205" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Asha - Lucknow</text><text x="680" y="240" font-size="16" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Neha - Lucknow</text></svg>
hints: Text values should be inside quotes.

questions.title: Sort Records with ORDER BY
questions.prompt: Write an SQL query to show all students sorted by age in ascending order.

examples.output: SELECT * FROM students ORDER BY age ASC;

questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1000 340" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="ORDER BY sorting"><rect width="1000" height="340" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.74)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="50" font-size="28" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">ORDER BY</text><text x="30" y="90" font-size="18" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">ORDER BY sorts the result.</text><rect x="70" y="130" width="560" height="90" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.16)"/><text x="100" y="185" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">SELECT * FROM students ORDER BY age ASC;</text><rect x="700" y="110" width="210" height="170" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.12)"/><text x="740" y="155" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">ASC</text><text x="740" y="195" font-size="18" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">18</text><text x="740" y="225" font-size="18" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">19</text><text x="740" y="255" font-size="18" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">20</text></svg>
questions.solution_code:
SELECT * FROM students
ORDER BY age ASC;

questions.title: Show Unique Cities
questions.prompt: Write an SQL query to show unique city names from the students table.
questions.svg_html: <svg viewBox="0 0 1000 340" width="100%" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" role="img" aria-label="DISTINCT unique values"><rect width="1000" height="340" rx="24" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.74)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.18)"/><text x="30" y="50" font-size="28" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">DISTINCT</text><text x="30" y="90" font-size="18" font-weight="700" fill="#5b6570">DISTINCT removes duplicate values from the result.</text><rect x="70" y="130" width="500" height="90" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,159,28,0.16)"/><text x="100" y="185" font-size="24" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">SELECT DISTINCT city FROM students;</text><rect x="660" y="115" width="250" height="170" rx="18" fill="rgba(255,255,255,0.9)" stroke="rgba(31,35,40,0.15)"/><text x="700" y="160" font-size="20" font-weight="900" fill="#1f2328">Unique Cities</text><text x="700" y="205" font-size="18" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Lucknow</text><text x="700" y="235" font-size="18" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Delhi</text><text x="700" y="265" font-size="18" font-weight="800" fill="#5b6570">Mumbai</text></svg>
